player: Real people. A fire. Someone cooking.
elias: They'll want to see your faces.
Don't look scared. They'll think you brought something with you.
I walked the last six miles with them.

guard: All right. Come on in. Don't touch anything.

player: They've been here a while. This is organized.

sam: You need food? I've got rabbit stew. Don't ask what it's stretched with.
Sit. You look like you've been walking for three days.
My husband was a hunter. Good one.
He went out three weeks ago and didn't come back.
His rifle's still up at the old lookout. End of the east trail, past the deadfall.
I'd get it myself but my knee's bad and I don't—
I just would rather it not stay up there.
You don't have to say that.

player: I found it. At the lookout.
sam: He'd been meaning to clean it.

tilly: Elias says you came through Blue Ridge in the dark.
tilly2: Come sit with us tonight. After dinner.
Nothing to do here but talk.

tilly: My grandmother was from Harlan County.
She said the mountains never change, only the people do.
She was right, she was just wrong about which direction the change went.
tilly2: Hard. Warm. Couldn't tell which one she was until you needed something.
I think about her a lot lately.
She'd have handled this better than I am.

tilly: I found this in an abandoned house outside of Buchanan.
Figured it was waiting for the right person.
If I wasn't sure I wouldn't have offered.
tilly2: Come back. After the mine. If you get through.

player: I can't take that. You might need it.
tilly: People need things. Doesn't mean they shouldn't give them.

rook: You shouldn't have made it this far.
No offense. The odds were not in your favor.
rook2: They mean they had help and didn't die.
That's not luck. That's just not dying.

rook: The mine goes four floors down.
Excavator-class bot on the bottom level. That's the boss of it.
Take it out and the upper floors go passive.
The captives are on floors two and three.
rook2: I've been watching it for ten days.
I was going to do something about it.

survivor_a: Heard there's a settlement out in Kansas that went voluntary.
Whole town converted. People say they're fine. Look fine.
Someone said they saw a man there tending a garden.
Said he looked familiar. Boston accent.

player: It doesn't feel safe to stay.
It never feels safe to stay.

elias: You don't have to go in.
Anyone who wants to stay at camp, stays.

rook: There's a secondary unit. Smaller than the Excavator. Call sign Warden Six.
Overseers say it shows up after the big one goes down.
If you beat the Excavator, assume it's coming.
